Explore the fleet

    Type to search · ↑↓ to move · Enter to open · Esc to close

    Home › Airlines › United Airlines

    UA United Airlines

    United States · Star Alliance · Hub: Chicago O'Hare (ORD)

    The largest airline fleet in the world — over a thousand mainline aircraft, and the only US carrier flying all three 787 variants.

    Types in fleet6
    Biggest aircraftBoeing 777
    AllianceStar Alliance
    Founded1926
    IATAUA
    Side by side

    The fleet at true scale

    Every type United Airlines flies, drawn to the same scale — widest wings on the left. Tap any silhouette.

    Boeing 777
    Boeing 787
    Boeing 767
    Boeing 757
    Boeing 737
    Airbus A320
    The fleet

    What United Airlines flies

    Grid
    96 in fleet

    Boeing 777

    Boeing · In production (777X in certification)

    The 777's launch operator, 1995 — still flying the original -200s domestically.

    Full specification →
    87 in fleet

    Boeing 787 Dreamliner

    Boeing · In production

    The world's largest 787 operator and the only US carrier flying all three variants: 12 × 787-8, 54 × 787-9, 21 × 787-10.

    Full specification →
    ≈37 in fleet

    Boeing 767

    Boeing · In production (freighter/tanker)

    Full specification →
    ≈40 in fleet

    Boeing 757

    Boeing · Out of production (2004) — in service

    Full specification →
    ≈390 in fleet

    Boeing 737

    Boeing · In production (MAX series)

    Full specification →
    ≈330 in fleet

    Airbus A320 family

    Airbus · In production

    Full specification →
    Route records

    Their longest flights

    RouteAircraftDistanceBlock time
    Houston (IAH) → Sydney (SYD)Boeing 787-913,834 km17 h 30

    From our longest flights in the world board.

    Good to know

    United Airlines, in a few facts

    • Rolling out free Starlink internet across its fleet — installations began in 2025.
    • Serves more destinations than any other airline in the world.
    Sources
    Primary sources
    • Airlines with the biggest fleets — Aerospace Global News, Dec 2025 ↗
    • Boeing 787 operator fleets — ch-aviation via Simple Flying, June 2026 ↗

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
